Party D (a financial buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.00 per share (subject to a four-week diligence period).

bid_claims.quote_text: At the effective time of the merger, each share of common stock issued and outstanding immediately prior to the effective time of the merger (including any preferred stock deemed to be converted into common stock, but excluding shares that are owned by G&W or any of its direct or indirect subsidiaries, including Merger Sub) will be canceled and will be converted automatically into the right to receive $25.00 in cash, without interest.

Between May 19, 2016 and June 1, 2016, the Company received nine written indications of interest (“IOIs”) from potential buyers, with offer prices per share ranging from $17.93 to $26.50 (assuming the conversion of preferred stock into common stock), implying equity values of $90 million to $134 million.
On July 12, 2016, Party C submitted an IOI with an offer price per share of $21.00 (implying an equity value of $108 million).
In late July 2016, the Company received six LOIs with offer prices per share ranging from $19.20 to $24.00 (implying equity values of $96 million to $121 million).
Party B submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $24.00 per share (subject to an expedited diligence review).
G&W submitted an LOI on July 21, 2016 to acquire the Company for a price of $21.15 per share (subject to a three-week exclusive diligence period).
The price included $20.02 cash at closing and $1.13 in the form of a contingent value right, the value of which was based on the subsequent sale of the Company’s South Quay property (“CVR”).
The price included $20.02 cash at closing and $1.13 in the form of a contingent value right, the value of which was based on the subsequent sale of the Company’s South Quay property (“CVR”).

On July 26, 2016, in response to feedback from representatives of GHF indicating its price and CVR structure were not competitive, G&W submitted a revised LOI, which increased its offer to $22.15 per share (including $21.02 in cash and $1.13 CVR).

event_claims.description: Merger Sub will merge with and into the Company, and the Company will survive as a wholly-owned subsidiary of G&W.
event_claims.quote_text: Under the terms and conditions set forth in the merger agreement, Merger Sub will merge with and into the Company, with the Company being the surviving corporation and becoming a wholly-owned subsidiary of G&W.

event_claims.description: Party A suggested joint venture arrangements and expressed interest in acquiring equity in the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: During the meeting, Party A suggested possible joint venture arrangements and expressed some interest in acquiring equity in the Company to further develop strategic opportunities and enhance regional connectivity.

event_claims.description: The Board approved retaining GHF, and the Company engaged GHF.
event_claims.quote_text: During the executive session of the Board’s regular quarterly meeting held on January 27, 2016 (which Director Alfred P. Smith did not attend), the subcommittee recommended to the Board that the Company retain GHF as its investment banking firm.

event_claims.description: GHF proposed a process for discussions with Party A and solicitation of other potential third parties for a change in control transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: At a special meeting of the Board held on March 14, 2016, representatives of GHF discussed their evaluation of the Company’s business operations and strategy for improving operational and financial performance, as well as railroad industry dynamics more generally and their impact on current railroad valuations.

event_claims.description: The Board appointed the Transaction Committee to assist in supervision and oversight of the process and make a recommendation to the full Board.
event_claims.quote_text: at the conclusion of the meeting, the Board appointed a special committee of independent directors of the Board (which we refer to as the “Transaction Committee”), composed of Frank W. Barrett (Chair), Richard W. Anderson and James C. Garvey, for the purpose of assisting in the supervision and oversight of the process on the full Board’s behalf and making a recommendation to the full Board.

event_claims.description: Company management met with Party A representatives on March 22 and March 23, 2016.
event_claims.quote_text: On March 22, 2016 and March 23, 2016, Daniel T. Noreck (the Company’s Chief Financial Officer and Treasurer) and other members of the senior management team of the Company met with various representatives of Party A.

event_claims.description: GHF was authorized to contact Party A and other potential buyers to assess interest in a change in control transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: The Transaction Committee authorized GHF to contact Party A as well as other potential buyers to determine their level of interest in a change in control transaction.

event_claims.description: GHF contacted 11 potential strategic buyers and 18 potential financial buyers.
event_claims.quote_text: During the week of March 28, 2016, in accordance with the Transaction Committee’s directives, representatives of GHF contacted 11 potential strategic buyers (including Party A) and 18 potential financial buyers.

event_claims.description: Each potential strategic buyer and 14 potential financial buyers executed confidentiality agreements.
event_claims.quote_text: Each of the potential strategic buyers and 14 potential financial buyers subsequently executed confidentiality agreements.

event_claims.description: GHF and management held introductory meetings with five potential strategic buyers, including G&W.
event_claims.quote_text: Between April 3, 2016 and April 6, 2016, representatives of GHF and members of the Company’s management held a series of introductory meetings with five potential strategic buyers (including G&W) at the American Short Line and Regional Railroad Association’s 2016 Connections Convention (“2016 Connections Convention”).

event_claims.description: The Company approached two additional Class I railroads to assess interest in a possible transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: Mr. Noreck reported that the Company had also approached two additional Class I railroads to determine their interest in a possible transaction.

event_claims.description: The Company and GHF held an introductory meeting with Party B.
event_claims.quote_text: Subsequently, on April 21, 2016, the Company and representatives of GHF held an introductory meeting with another potential strategic buyer (“Party B”).

event_claims.description: Potential buyers that had executed confidentiality agreements received a memorandum concerning the Company and its prospects.
event_claims.quote_text: With the assistance of GHF, the Company prepared a memorandum concerning the Company and its prospects, which was distributed to the potential buyers, including Party A, Party B and G&W, that had executed confidentiality agreements.

event_claims.description: Potential buyers were advised to submit non-binding indications of interest by a deadline that was later postponed to May 19, 2016.
event_claims.quote_text:
On April 27, 2016,

28

the Board met in executive session (without Mr. Eder present) with representatives of GHF and discussed the status of the process, including that each potential buyer had been advised to submit a non-binding indication of interest by May 10, 2016 (which deadline was subsequently postponed to May 19, 2016 to ensure the potential buyers had sufficient time to formulate their most compelling offers).

event_claims.description: The Company received nine written indications of interest from potential buyers between May 19, 2016 and June 1, 2016.
event_claims.quote_text: Between May 19, 2016 and June 1, 2016, the Company received nine written indications of interest (“IOIs”) from potential buyers, with offer prices per share ranging from $17.93 to $26.50 (assuming the conversion of preferred stock into common stock), implying equity values of $90 million to $134 million.

event_claims.description: The Transaction Committee concluded that the two low bidders should be excluded from management presentations.
event_claims.quote_text: In view of the substantial amount of management time that would be required for management presentations, the Transaction Committee concluded that the two low bidders should be excluded from that process.

event_claims.description: The Transaction Committee authorized management presentations and additional due diligence for the remaining seven potential buyers.
event_claims.quote_text: The Transaction Committee authorized representatives of GHF to schedule in-person management presentations with the remaining seven potential buyers and allow these parties to conduct additional due diligence.

event_claims.description: Potential buyers were instructed in mid-June 2016 to submit non-binding LOIs by July 20, 2016.
event_claims.quote_text: In mid-June 2016, at the direction of the Transaction Committee, representatives of GHF instructed all potential buyers to submit non-binding letters of intent (“LOIs”) by July 20, 2016, together with mark-ups of the forms of merger agreement, disclosure letter and voting agreement, which GHF indicated would be posted to the internet data site.

event_claims.description: Party C approached Company management and expressed interest in acquiring the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: In early July 2016, a potential strategic buyer that had not previously been part of the process (“Party C”) approached the Company’s management and expressed interest in acquiring the Company.

event_claims.description: Party C submitted an indication of interest on July 12, 2016.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 12, 2016, Party C submitted an IOI with an offer price per share of $21.00 (implying an equity value of $108 million).

event_claims.description: Party C received data site access and a management presentation by telephone.
event_claims.quote_text: Subsequently, Party C was provided access to the internet data site and the Company’s management held a management presentation with Party C by telephone on July 14, 2016.

event_claims.description: The Company received six LOIs in late July 2016.
event_claims.quote_text: In late July 2016, the Company received six LOIs with offer prices per share ranging from $19.20 to $24.00 (implying equity values of $96 million to $121 million).

event_claims.description: G&W submitted an LOI on July 21, 2016.
event_claims.quote_text: G&W submitted an LOI on July 21, 2016 to acquire the Company for a price of $21.15 per share (subject to a three-week exclusive diligence period).

event_claims.description: G&W submitted a revised LOI on July 26, 2016.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 26, 2016, in response to feedback from representatives of GHF indicating its price and CVR structure were not competitive, G&W submitted a revised LOI, which increased its offer to $22.15 per share (including $21.02 in cash and $1.13 CVR).

event_claims.description: Party B submitted an LOI to acquire the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: Party B submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $24.00 per share (subject to an expedited diligence review).

event_claims.description: Party E submitted an LOI to acquire the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: Party E (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.26 per share (subject to a 60-day exclusivity period for due diligence and negotiation of definitive documentation).

event_claims.description: Party D submitted an LOI to acquire the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: Party D (a financial buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.00 per share (subject to a four-week diligence period).

event_claims.description: Party C submitted an LOI to acquire the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: Party C submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $19.30 per share (subject to a 30-day diligence period).

event_claims.description: Party F submitted an LOI to acquire the Company.
event_claims.quote_text: Party F (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $19.20 per share (subject to a 30-day due diligence period).

event_claims.actor_label: one strategic buyer and one financial buyer
event_claims.description: One strategic buyer and one financial buyer did not submit an LOI.
event_claims.quote_text: One strategic buyer and one financial buyer elected not to submit an LOI.

event_claims.description: The Transaction Committee concluded that the Company should proceed with confirmatory due diligence and negotiations with G&W and Party B.
event_claims.quote_text: The Transaction Committee concluded that the Company should proceed with confirmatory due diligence and negotiations with G&W and Party B because of the higher offers made by each of G&W and Party B relative to the other potential buyers.

event_claims.description: GHF informed the remaining bidders they were no longer involved in the process.
event_claims.quote_text: At the direction of the Transaction Committee, representatives of GHF subsequently contacted the remaining bidders to inform them that they were no longer involved in the process.

event_claims.description: Party D expressed interest in reengaging and enhancing its offer.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 29, 2016, representatives of GHF had calls with Party D and Party E. During those calls, Party D and Party E both expressed interest in reengaging and enhancing their offers.

event_claims.description: Party E expressed interest in reengaging and enhancing its offer.
event_claims.quote_text: On July 29, 2016, representatives of GHF had calls with Party D and Party E. During those calls, Party D and Party E both expressed interest in reengaging and enhancing their offers.

event_claims.description: Party D submitted a revised LOI on August 1, 2016.
event_claims.quote_text: On August 1, 2016, Party D submitted a revised LOI at a price of $24.00 per share and Party E submitted a revised LOI, along with financing sup
